title: Two-phase distiller: gather->basis, then synthesize->brief
userStory: As the resolver, I want to obtain the distill basis from the recall gather alone — before any LLM call — so the Tier 1 gate can skip synthesis when the brain moved but the company-fit-relevant content did not, without re-running recall.
acceptanceCriteria: distill.Distiller exposes a two-phase API: a Gather step that runs only the recall fan-out + dedup and returns the gathered chunks plus the stable basis (the existing basisOf over both prompts + chunk content), and a Synthesize step that takes the gathered result and runs classify + synthesize to produce the brief. Distill() is reimplemented as Gather-then-Synthesize so `scout distill` and Refresh() keep identical end-to-end behavior
The basis returned by Gather is byte-identical to the basis Distill() returns for the same recall result (the split must not change the hash) — verified by reusing the same basisOf path

The criteria.BriefSource interface (internal/criteria/resolver.go) is widened to expose the gather/basis phase separately from synthesis, keeping it implementable by a test fake without a live brain or LLM. Document the interface contract: empty brief + nil error still means 'brain genuinely knows nothing'; any error distinguishes outage from empty
No second recall fan-out: a single Resolve that needs synthesis performs exactly one recall gather, then synthesis — not a Basis() recall followed by a Distill() recall

[Verification] go test ./internal/distill/... and ./internal/criteria/... pass. A unit test asserts: (a) Gather then Synthesize yields the same brief as Distill for a stubbed recall+LLM; (b) Synthesize is NOT invoked when the caller decides the basis is unchanged (use a fake/counter). `scout distill --brainbot <url>` still prints chunks + brief unchanged.
